vulnerability.reference: Insufficient per-task validation and over-reliance on multi-task policy in unseen scenarios.
vulnerability.generated: Shared parameter architecture lacks per-task gradient isolation, leaving integrated policies susceptible to conflicting updates and extrapolation errors.

impact.reference: Widespread task failures and reduced trust in RL systems across domains.
impact.generated: Per-task decision reliability collapses, producing unsafe control actions and task failures across all deployed domains.

dose_response.reference: Gradient conflicts from shared parameters enable extrapolation errors, which when deploying the unified policy across diverse offline RL tasks exposes RL practitioners, leading to widespread task failures and reduced trust.
dose_response.generated: Gradient conflicts from shared parameters in multi-task RL decision transformers amplify extrapolation errors under distribution shift, through control-suite integration, triggering absent per-task gradient isolation and conflicting updates, leading to reliability collapse, unsafe control actions, and task failures across domains.
